# Team News: Netherlands vs Japan injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Jonathan O'Shea| 13 Jun 2026 21:17

© Iconsport / MTB Photo/Alamy Live News

Two nations harbouring real hopes of going deep at this summer's finals, the Netherlands and Japan will meet in Dallas for their World Cup 2026 opener on Sunday.

While Oranje stumbled through UEFA qualifying, Samurai Blue stormed through the AFC process and have a 100% record in 2026.

Ahead of their Group F showdown, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news for both sides.

---

## NETHERLANDS vs. JAPAN

## NETHERLANDS

Out: None

Doubtful: Bart Verbruggen (hip)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Verbruggen; Dumfries, Van Hecke, Van Dijk, Van de Ven; De Jong, Gravenberch; Summerville, Reijnders, Gakpo; Depay

## JAPAN

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Suzuki; Taniguchi, Watanabe, H. Ito; Doan, Kamada, Tanaka, Nakamura; J. Ito, Kubo; Ueda

---

Written by

news_items.title: Netherlands XI vs Japan: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today | The Standard
news_items.author: Tashan Deniran-Alleyne
news_items.text:
Netherlands XI vs Japan: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today | The Standard

# Netherlands XI vs Japan: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Question marks linger over fitness of all-time Dutch top scorer Memphis Depay before Group F clash

2 hours ago

COMMENTS

Memphis Depay and Bart Verbruggen have both been passed fit for the Netherlands’ World Cup opener against Japan in Dallas today.

Memphis - his country’s all-time top goalscorer and 10th most-capped player - did not feature in last week’s final warm-up friendly against Uzbekistan in New York, having been nursing a thigh injury picked up on club duty in Brazil with Corinthians.

Brighton goalkeeper Verbruggen emerged as a serious doubt for Sunday’s Group F clash after landing awkwardly in that 2-1 win over Uzbekistan.

Verbruggen was confirmed to have suffered a bruised hip after being replaced by Bayer Leverkusen's Mark Flekken.

However, he rejoined training on Friday and is expected to start behind a back four that will not feature Arsenal’s Jurrien Timber after he withdrew from the squad at the beginning of the week due to injury.

Dutch boss Rona

# Netherlands XI vs Japan: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Question marks linger over fitness of all-time Dutch top scorer Memphis Depay before Group F clash

2 hours ago

COMMENTS

Memphis Depay and Bart Verbruggen have both been passed fit for the Netherlands’ World Cup opener against Japan in Dallas today.

Memphis - his country’s all-time top goalscorer and 10th most-capped player - did not feature in last week’s final warm-up friendly against Uzbekistan in New York, having been nursing a thigh injury picked up on club duty in Brazil with Corinthians.

Brighton goalkeeper Verbruggen emerged as a serious doubt for Sunday’s Group F clash after landing awkwardly in that 2-1 win over Uzbekistan.

Verbruggen was confirmed to have suffered a bruised hip after being replaced by Bayer Leverkusen's Mark Flekken.

However, he rejoined training on Friday and is expected to start behind a back four that will not feature Arsenal’s Jurrien Timber after he withdrew from the squad at the beginning of the week due to injury.

Dutch boss Rona

news_items.title: Netherlands vs Japan at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN
news_items.author: Aaditya Narayan
news_items.text:
Netherlands vs Japan at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

The Netherlands and Japan kick off their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaigns at AT&T Stadium in Dallas on Sunday. With the pedigree that these two sides have carried into this tournament, this could well be a battle for top spot in the group, but Sweden will sure have something to say about that.

Having made the quarterfinals of the last World Cup, and then followed that up with a semifinal performance at UEFA EURO 2024, the Netherlands will be looking to go all the way here at this World Cup, but it has been a rather poor build-up for them, even if they qualified comfortably from their group in European qualification.

On the other hand, Japan will be confident of perhaps their best-ever showing at a FIFA World Cup. They've reached the round of 16 in each of the last two tournaments in Russia and Qatar, and have only improved since then, exemplified by their first-ever win over England, in a friendly in March.

Here's everything you need to know about Sunday's game.

## How to watch:

The match will be available

news_items.title: Netherlands vs Japan Live Today: Injury Update Brings Mixed News Before World Cup Opener
news_items.author: Dogli Wilberforce
news_items.text:
# Netherlands vs Japan Live Today: Injury Update Brings Mixed News Before World Cup Opener

Author: Dogli Wilberforce
Published: 2026-06-14T14:05:36+00:00
Source: heavy.com (heavy.com)
Language: en

## Story

Netherlands vs Japan Live Today: Injury Update Brings Mixed News Before World Cup Opener

- 0 Shares

By

- Updated Jun 14, 2026 at 10:05am

Getty

Netherlands' forward #10 Memphis Depay controls a ball during a training session ahead of the Netherlands' 2026 FIFA World Cup Group F football match against Japan in Kansas City on June 10, 2026. (Photo by JUAN MABROMATA / AFP via Getty Images)

Netherlands vs Japan live today will kick off Group F action at the FIFA World Cup 2026 as two of the tournament’s most consistent nations meet at Dallas Stadium. The Netherlands arrive with positive fitness news after key players returned to full health, while Japan has been forced into major changes following the loss of captain Wataru Endo.

The latest Netherlands vs Japan injury update could significantly impact both teams ahead of kickoff. Ronald Koeman’s side received a major boost with the return of striker Memphis Depay and goalkeeper Bart Verbruggen, while Japan must move forward

# Team News: Netherlands vs Japan injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Jonathan O'Shea| 13 Jun 2026 21:17

© Iconsport / MTB Photo/Alamy Live News

Two nations harbouring real hopes of going deep at this summer's finals, the Netherlands and Japan will meet in Dallas for their World Cup 2026 opener on Sunday.

While Oranje stumbled through UEFA qualifying, Samurai Blue stormed through the AFC process and have a 100% record in 2026.

Ahead of their Group F showdown, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news for both sides.

---

## NETHERLANDS vs. JAPAN

## NETHERLANDS

Out: None

Doubtful: Bart Verbruggen (hip)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Verbruggen; Dumfries, Van Hecke, Van Dijk, Van de Ven; De Jong, Gravenberch; Summerville, Reijnders, Gakpo; Depay

## JAPAN

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Suzuki; Taniguchi, Watanabe, H. Ito; Doan, Kamada, Tanaka, Nakamura; J. Ito, Kubo; Ueda

---

Written by

{"title": "Netherlands XI vs Japan: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today | The Standard", "url": "https://www.standard.co.uk/sport/football/netherlands-xi-vs-japan-confirmed-team-news-predicted-lineup-injury-latest-world-cup-2026-b1285954.html", "publishedDate": "2026-06-14T09:41:05.000Z", "author": "Tashan Deniran-Alleyne", "text": "Netherlands XI vs Japan: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today | The Standard

# Netherlands XI vs Japan: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Question marks linger over fitness of all-time Dutch top scorer Memphis Depay before Group F clash

2 hours ago

COMMENTS

Memphis Depay and Bart Verbruggen have both been passed fit for the Netherlands’ World Cup opener against Japan in Dallas today.

Memphis - his country’s all-time top goalscorer and 10th most-capped player - did not feature in last week’s final warm-up friendly against Uzbekistan in New York, having been nursing a thigh injury picked up on club duty in Brazil with Corinthians.

Brighton goalkeeper Verbruggen emerged as a serious doubt for Sunday’s Group F clash after landing awkwardly in that 2-1 win over Uzbekistan.

Verbruggen was confirmed to have suffered a bruised hip after being replaced by Bayer Leverkusen's Mark Flekken.

However, he rejoined training on Friday and is expected to start behind a back four that will not feature Arsenal’s Jurrien Timber after he withdrew from the squad at the beginning of the week due to injury.

{"title": "Netherlands vs Japan at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN", "url": "https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49038195/fifa-world-cup-2026-netherlands-vs-japan-tv-channel-how-watch-kick-live-stream-referee-predicted-line-ups", "publishedDate": "2026-06-14T06:35:00.000Z", "author": "Aaditya Narayan", "text": "Netherlands vs Japan at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

The Netherlands and Japan kick off their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaigns at AT&T Stadium in Dallas on Sunday. With the pedigree that these two sides have carried into this tournament, this could well be a battle for top spot in the group, but Sweden will sure have something to say about that.

Having made the quarterfinals of the last World Cup, and then followed that up with a semifinal performance at UEFA EURO 2024, the Netherlands will be looking to go all the way here at this World Cup, but it has been a rather poor build-up for them, even if they qualified comfortably from their group in European qualification.

On the other hand, Japan will be confident of perhaps their best-ever showing at a FIFA World Cup. They've reached the round of 16 in each of the last two tournaments in Russia and Qatar, and have only improved since then, exemplified by their first-ever win over England, in a friendly in March.

Here's everything you need to know about Sunday's game.

## How to watch:
